alt="Your browser understands the <APPLET> tag but isn't running the applet, for some reason." Your browser is completely ignoring the <APPLET> tag! Who left me here? email: bwang@molsci.org

I have been working with Andy Mendelsohn, using protein interaction methods to explore the connection between the regulation of cell division and cell death.

In present, a collection of proteins involved in cell cycle regulation and apoptosis are being investigated. The detection of the interactions is facilitated by construction of mating panels. In short, we use two yeast strains of different mating types (alpha and a). They are transformed with plasmids that express proteins of interest fused to either Lex A or B42 (baits or preys). By mating, the interacting protein complex can turn on transcription of a reporter gene, which allows us to identify the interaction.

 

Future Interests:

Bioinformatics

Engineering a "killing" yeast strain

Frequency selection & complex human behaviors in stock market
